stages.name: task_system_validation
stages.evidence: validate_failure_recovery now runs inside the task-system validator; the six valid failure-recovery fixtures pass and all eight invalid fixtures are rejected with stable, project-relative diagnostics.

stages.name: failure_recovery_validation
stages.evidence: validate_failure_recovery_record enforces the docs/FAILURE_RECOVERY.md state machine over zentinel.pipeline.failure_recovery_transition.v1: schema_version/task_id/state/trigger/evidence structure; retry.limit must match the documented Retry Limits table for its task class; a flaky result is never waived; rollback evidence must be scope=agent_owned with a non-empty project-relative changed_files list; a rollback_required state without that evidence is rejected; a failure state never transitions to complete (canonical failed_mutation_gate -> complete rejected); retry.cycle exceeding retry.limit may only transition to escalated; and every (from,trigger,to) triple must be in the documented transition table. The RED capture (stub record validator) showed all eight invalid fixtures wrongly accepted before the rules landed.

stages.name: unit_tests
stages.evidence: no Zig source changed (src/** and test/**/*.zig are forbidden for this task); the full Zig test suite is unaffected and remains green.

stages.name: pipeline_artifact_validation
stages.evidence: the committed artifacts/pipeline tree (now including artifacts/pipeline/065/verification/report.json) validates with no violations; the ci_artifacts self-test still passes.

stages.name: mutation_checks
stages.reason: task 065 changes only scripts/validate_task_system.py (a structural validator), JSON fixtures, and recovery/lifecycle docs; no product code changed, so mutation testing is not applicable. The final release dogfood gate is task 085.

residual_risk: The failure-recovery validator is a structural check over zentinel.pipeline.failure_recovery_transition.v1 records and their fixtures; it does not itself execute recovery (no automated rollback commands, no git history rewriting -- both non-goals). It enforces that recorded transitions are documented and auditable.

Recovery transition records are not yet emitted by a running pipeline; the validator and fixtures define and enforce the contract so that when real recovery artifacts appear they cannot mark a failed gate complete or hide retry exhaustion.

rollback.changed_files captures agent-owned edits as project-relative paths so they are distinguished from pre-existing user edits; the validator rejects user_owned scope and missing changed-file evidence, but it cannot by itself prove a listed path was truly agent-authored -- that remains the recording agent's auditable responsibility.
